| | NEGOP Unicameral Update"2ND HOUSE ALERT"
NEGOP Legislative Update for Week ending January 20th, 2023 These are very exciting times to be living through at the Capitol. Party membership passed a bold yet common-sense legislative agenda in August 2022 for the legislative session in 2023. We are seeing those items blooming in bill form at the state house! The legislative week started with a bang when the Democrats tried to adjourn the ENTIRETY OF THE LEGISLATIVE SESSION on Tuesday to halt all progress and work currently being done. The bottom line, this is probably your best signal of the progress of the 2023 session. We witnessed Speaker Arch and numerous Senators from both parties scurry out of a public presentation in downtown Lincoln without notice to keep the session intact. Credit to newly appointed Senator Ballard for following parliamentary procedure (Call of the House) to preserve the time to achieve a correct vote. The last day to file a bill was Wednesday. We have over 800 bills to consider for this session. Speaker Arch wisely spaced a gap of a week between bills being filed and committee hearings. As many of you are aware, we have transitioned as a party to a “ground-up” approach. Our Central Committee members who have played an invaluable and critical role in their various subcommittees on key issues are still in process of reviewing bills and providing feedback. We look forward to providing more guidance on specific bills next week once consensus is achieved on issues that are not clearly defined in our adopted source documents. However, we have you covered by notifying you of key committee hearings through the end of the month. Here is the recap on the big news (after the adjournment attempt) for the week: Taxes Last week we saw the big proposal on life and this week we saw the big proposal for Tax Relief which is sorely needed for competitiveness and prosperity in Nebraska. A large part of the surplus on hand at the state level is being used to lower income taxes and provide property tax relief via assuming expenditures of education at the state level. The governing body of the party will be taking a formal position on the Consumption tax this weekend. We will update more on the consumption tax next week based on the SCC vote. Income Taxes Big strides are being made by Senator Linehan in conjunction with Governor Pillen to accelerate tax reductions that were already passed. We are excited that our Seniors are being served by ending the taxes on social security in 2024 (if passed). This is a big step to helping our Seniors keep their wisdom at home to help future generations. Furthermore, tax cuts passed last year designed to take effect in future years, will be accelerated with all cuts taking their full effect in 2024 (if passed). The political team is very excited about this as our competitiveness was in danger with cuts by surrounding states. Furthermore, relief in the wallet was needed! We will have a full percentage point decrease in corporate and income taxes in place for 2024 if passed. Furthermore, bills are in the works to drop the income tax rate to 3.99% by 2027. Again, this keeps us competitive with neighboring states as the battle for labor is very real as states like Iowa have dropped their income tax rate by over 50%. Our citizens deserve more of the fruits of their labor. Property Taxes
Property Taxes will be shrinking as the state seeks to assume more of the educational spending from local governments. The big accomplishment in the education/property relief package was Senator Briese’s proposal to cap expenditure growth. The proposal finally addresses the game of levy AND value that local taxing boards have played for years. We have a cap of 3% plus growth (percentage growth of student population) in schools UNLESS a vote of 75% of the school board or 60% of the people occurs. This is a high threshold, but yet an unfortunately likely threshold for many of our urban cities based on current progressive strongholds over these bodies. Second, the bill addresses special education funding which was a big problem for schools and had to be dealt with. Finally, despite tax relief, overall spending on the same systems was insured and their budgets will not be limited if they can obtain the votes of a local school board. Long-term tax relief can only happen when expenditure control occurs. The systems still received their assurances of more money so we will have to monitor school boards and educational spending moving forward. Nonetheless, this was a BIG step in the right direction to achieving property tax relief. We are happy to see the surplus being used to achieve this goal. Education As we discussed earlier, we are worried about our competitiveness with surrounding states for labor and educational performance. Income tax is being addressed but families move for education as well. Our two largest school districts illustrate what is happening. We see the migration out of Omaha Public Schools to Elkhorn. We see the creation of new charter schools and waiting lists in Lincoln private schools to leave LPS as evidenced by their enrollment numbers. Citizens are voting with their feet. The performance scores do not make it a mystery of why parents are moving their children. We think the right thing to do is to give parents more options instead of being forced to move.
It was a tough week for those seeking choices in their schooling options. Linnehan has an excellent bill to provide scholarships, but the state party is looking for more robust and bigger options in Choice to complement good bills like these. Fortunately, our Central Committee members have been working with [Senator Erdman on a robust choice bill called My Child, My Choice Act ](https://www.ne.gop/so/f2ONQC46j/c?w=V4piaybUUZikr8Ymh_X6PvqQeePF88Ufu8fDxoFIIFI.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmVicmFza2FsZWdpc2xhdHVyZS5nb3YvRmxvb3JEb2NzLzEwOC9QREYvSW50cm8vTEIxNzcucGRmIiwiciI6IjY5YzViNzZhLTg0OTItNDI1Yy05Njk1LTEyZWU5ZGRhNWQzNCIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6IjQxOGMxNzYxLWVkZDMtNGMxYS1iMDY0LWMwYmFjOWQwMjY0ZiJ9)to support our legislative plan passed in August. The party fully supports investing in EVERY child (which includes private school kids). However, we have a difference of opinion on how to invest. We believe investments need to be placed into the hands of the parent and not a system with falling scores who have already proven their ability to steward more money for academic results (not working). Competition works. We trust parents’ investing choices for their most valuable asset...their children. [ LB177 ](https://www.ne.gop/so/f2ONQC46j/c?w=aX9d1pakwKKEOEuTF0fplrAtUUDJr5q7nVzZu4kgzFI.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9uZWJyYXNrYWxlZ2lzbGF0dXJlLmdvdi9GbG9vckRvY3MvMTA4L1BERi9JbnRyby9MQjE3Ny5wZGYiLCJyIjoiNjljNWI3NmEtODQ5Mi00MjVjLTk2OTUtMTJlZTlkZGE1ZDM0IiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiNDE4YzE3NjEtZWRkMy00YzFhLWIwNjQtYzBiYWM5ZDAyNjRmIn0)is an amazing bill to start to achieve that goal. Changes will happen to a bill to pass, but the bold vision and legal framework is there to make this happen. The Iowa legislature in conjunction with their Governor beat us to the punch again this week by passing out of committee a bill that would invest over 7k in funds to private school funds. This tips the scales of competitiveness back to Iowa. That can’t happen. Secondarily, the Parental Rights bill from Senator Murman was introduced. Parents and not systems have the final say in their children. [LB 374](https://www.ne.gop/so/f2ONQC46j/c?w=ZybFHB_dO3xkRdl9SiviJAhmJnGjkRthllD7BDHYxV0.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9uZWJyYXNrYWxlZ2lzbGF0dXJlLmdvdi9GbG9vckRvY3MvMTA4L1BERi9JbnRyby9MQjM3NC5wZGYiLCJyIjoiNjljNWI3NmEtODQ5Mi00MjVjLTk2OTUtMTJlZTlkZGE1ZDM0IiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiNDE4YzE3NjEtZWRkMy00YzFhLWIwNjQtYzBiYWM5ZDAyNjRmIn0) is a BIG step towards reestablishing the right relationship between child, parent, and school system. The hearing for this critical bill is on the afternoon of January 31st. Be there!
Finally, the SBOE continues to evaluate new candidates to lead the board of education. We are listening to our trusted Republican board members and watching these proceedings carefully. Our leadership has lived in a town with an ongoing trend of lower test scores and recently observed questionable teacher courses that must be observed by faculty. Lincoln’s current SuperIntendent was identified by the same "Search Firm" who is conducting the search for the leader of our state. Last week, a news story broke out of Sioux City [former-sioux-city-schools-superintendent-dr-gausman-suing-district-board-members](https://www.ne.gop/so/f2ONQC46j/c?w=xbkalAjdMopNLew6Es4Q7_ll_1cd8Kf3Nk3zK5GOOlI.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cua3Rpdi5jb20vMjAyMy8wMS8xNC9mb3JtZXItc2lvdXgtY2l0eS1zY2hvb2xzLXN1cGVyaW50ZW5kZW50LWRyLWdhdXNtYW4tc3VpbmctZGlzdHJpY3QtYm9hcmQtbWVtYmVycy8iLCJyIjoiNjljNWI3NmEtODQ5Mi00MjVjLTk2OTUtMTJlZTlkZGE1ZDM0IiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiNDE4YzE3NjEtZWRkMy00YzFhLWIwNjQtYzBiYWM5ZDAyNjRmIn0) that Lincoln’s current SuperIntendent is involved in a lawsuit with members of his former school board. The lawsuit centers around performance evaluations, bribery accusations of the Superintendent, and violations of the open meeting act. We will not speculate on the facts of the situation. However, we do feel a search firm should be screening candidates that have overwhelming support from their existing elected board AND possess a strong record of score improvement from the district they come from. We need to avoid the controversy that is surrounding local educational leadership in the next state leader. The search firm is paid a lot of money to screen candidates. Severe conflicts in the prior position should easily be caught by a search firm. Finally, I think we are concerned a lot of state policy input is coming from this individual with these types of complaints and accusations being made by multiple former school board officials. Our advice is to let due process play out, but avoid statewide policy input until the facts of this case are established in court. Stay tuned. Broadband The legislature, PSC, and Governor’s office continue to work on the issue of broadband. Communication on where each entity stands on this subject is in an ongoing dialogue. Our legislative plan and our Governor both echo the goal of accomplishing this huge task for the future of our state. It is imperative all sides continue to work together to accomplish this expensive but very important goal. It is a monumental but important undertaking by the legislature, Governor, and PSC.
ESG Efforts to protect our power plants, agriculture, and industry from non-financial ESG policies continue. Last week we had one bill. This week we have grown to 3 bills in this critical area. The work between the legislature, party, and Treasurer has been great. State leaders in West Virginia, North Dakota, and Texas have been instrumental in helping our state address this quickly evolving area. We thank them for working together! Rules We fielded a lot of calls this week that "the adopted Permanent Rules DID NOT uphold the Constitution". Citizens, we hear you loud and clear! We have expressed the need to follow the Constitution which is the rules the people place on the lawmakers before the lawmakers place rules over the people. However, the issue is not settled. As referenced in the Omaha World-Herald, Senator Erdman said the issue will be addressed later in the session. We know many Senators gave their word (and signature) to support transparency in committee votes. We don’t believe (but could be wrong) that they want to see the sun set on that commitment they made with their name behind it. The opportunity was there to object, but we will be patient and permanently judge this action or inaction at the end of the session. Constitutional Carry The hearing is set for January 26th at 1:30. There is continued dialogue between Senators at higher levels to secure the final votes. We are not privy to those conversations to provide you with details at this time. Protecting Women’s Sports Our Central committee expressed a desire to preserve women’s sports and the opportunities they present for our citizens. Senator Kauth filed that bill this week [(LB 575)](https://www.ne.gop/so/f2ONQC46j/c?w=nHxKnRW0n4vmWzqAiSYBM5HN4QpjTeys3q1UAaTVKZg.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9uZWJyYXNrYWxlZ2lzbGF0dXJlLmdvdi9GbG9vckRvY3MvMTA4L1BERi9JbnRyby9MQjU3NS5wZGYiLCJyIjoiNjljNWI3NmEtODQ5Mi00MjVjLTk2OTUtMTJlZTlkZGE1ZDM0IiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiNDE4YzE3NjEtZWRkMy00YzFhLWIwNjQtYzBiYWM5ZDAyNjRmIn0) in conjunction with her other bill addressing hormone therapy for kids [(LB574](https://www.ne.gop/so/f2ONQC46j/c?w=TL6djxAz1MuTm_F65WKetaV7N8W_2Kx0gJE7zULduB0.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9uZWJyYXNrYWxlZ2lzbGF0dXJlLmdvdi9GbG9vckRvY3MvMTA4L1BERi9JbnRyby9MQjU3NC5wZGYiLCJyIjoiNjljNWI3NmEtODQ5Mi00MjVjLTk2OTUtMTJlZTlkZGE1ZDM0IiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiNDE4YzE3NjEtZWRkMy00YzFhLWIwNjQtYzBiYWM5ZDAyNjRmIn0)). Both Senator Kauth and Senator Hansen have engaged the areas of healthcare with bills on directed health measures, medical freedom, and conscience issues being addressed. We are grateful for their leadership on issues that often take center stage with an unbalanced media. I want to thank everyone for the incredible work you are doing for the party and for Nebraska. Thank you for engaging in the political space with us. We could not do this without you. In particular, our Central Committee members have played invaluable and critical roles in their various subcommittees on key issues, organized by our Legislative Director, William Feely. They are still in the process of reviewing bills and providing feedback. We look forward to providing more guidance on specific bills next week. We hope to have a consensus achieved on issues that are not clearly defined in our adopted source documents. However, we are prepared to notify you of key committee hearings through the end of the month. Thank you!
Sincerely,

/s/Todd WatsonPolitical DirectorNEGOP

clean_body: For Your Situational Awareness PRIORITY BILLS : -The Constitutional Carry hearing is set for January 26th at 1:30 -Parents' Bill of Rights a | | Can't see this message? | | | | | | | 108th Legislative Session"2ND HOUSE ALERT/WEEKLY UPDATE"January 21, 2023 | | | | | | | For Your Situational AwarenessPRIORITY BILLS :-The Constitutional Carry hearing is set for January 26th at 1:30 -Parents' Bill of Rights and Academic Transparency Act Hearing on the afternoon of January 31stNOW IS THE TIME TO FILL THE SEATS OF THE "2ND HOUSE"! | | | | | NEGOP Unicameral Update"2ND HOUSE ALERT" NEGOP Legislative Update for Week ending January 20th, 2023 These are very exciting times to be living through at the Capitol. Party membership passed a bold yet common-sense legislative agenda in August 2022 for the legislative session in 2023. We are seeing those items blooming in bill form at the state house! The legislative week started with a bang when the Democrats tried to adjourn the ENTIRETY OF THE LEGISLATIVE SESSION on Tuesday to halt all progress and work currently being done. The bottom line, this is probably your best signal of the progress of the 2023 session. We witnessed Speaker Arch and numerous Senators from both parties scurry out of a public presentation in downtown Lincoln without notice to keep the session intact. Credit to newly appointed Senator Ballard for following parliamentary procedure (Call of the House) to preserve the time to achieve a correct vote. The last day to file a bill was Wednesday. We have over 800 bills to consider for this session. Speaker Arch wisely spaced a gap of a week between bills being filed and committee hearings. As many of you are aware, we have transitioned as a party to a “ground-up” approach. Our Central Committee members who have played an invaluable and critical role in their various subcommittees on key issues are still in process of reviewing bills and providing feedback. We look forward to providing more guidance on specific bills next week once consensus is achieved on issues that are not clearly defined in our adopted source documents. However, we have you covered by notifying you of key committee hearings through the end of the month. Here is the recap on the big news (after the adjournment attempt) for the week: Taxes Last week we saw the big proposal on life and this week we saw the big proposal for Tax Relief which is sorely needed for competitiveness and prosperity in Nebraska. A large part of the surplus on hand at the state level is being used to lower income taxes and provide property tax relief via assuming expenditures of education at the state level. The governing body of the party will be taking a formal position on the Consumption tax this weekend. We will update more on the consumption tax next week based on the SCC vote. Income Taxes Big strides are being made by Senator Linehan in conjunction with Governor Pillen to accelerate tax reductions that were already passed. We are excited that our Seniors are being served by ending the taxes on social security in 2024 (if passed). This is a big step to helping our Seniors keep their wisdom at home to help future generations. Furthermore, tax cuts passed last year designed to take effect in future years, will be accelerated with all cuts taking their full effect in 2024 (if passed). The political team is very excited about this as our competitiveness was in danger with cuts by surrounding states. Furthermore, relief in the wallet was needed! We will have a full percentage point decrease in corporate and income taxes in place for 2024 if passed. Furthermore, bills are in the works to drop the income tax rate to 3.99% by 2027. Again, this keeps us competitive with neighboring states as the battle for labor is very real as states like Iowa have dropped their income tax rate by over 50%. Our citizens deserve more of the fruits of their labor. Property Taxes Property Taxes will be shrinking as the state seeks to assume more of the educational spending from local governments. The big accomplishment in the education/property relief package was Senator Briese’s proposal to cap expenditure growth. The proposal finally addresses the game of levy AND value that local taxing boards have played for years. We have a cap of 3% plus growth (percentage growth of student population) in schools UNLESS a vote of 75% of the school board or 60% of the people occurs. This is a high threshold, but yet an unfortunately likely threshold for many of our urban cities based on current progressive strongholds over these bodies. Second, the bill addresses special education funding which was a big problem for schools and had to be dealt with. Finally, despite tax relief, overall spending on the same systems was insured and their budgets will not be limited if they can obtain the votes of a local school board. Long-term tax relief can only happen when expenditure control occurs. The systems still received their assurances of more money so we will have to monitor school boards and educational spending moving forward. Nonetheless, this was a BIG step in the right direction to achieving property tax relief. We are happy to see the surplus being used to achieve this goal. Education As we discussed earlier, we are worried about our competitiveness with surrounding states for labor and educational performance. Income tax is being addressed but families move for education as well. Our two largest school districts illustrate what is happening. We see the migration out of Omaha Public Schools to Elkhorn. We see the creation of new charter schools and waiting lists in Lincoln private schools to leave LPS as evidenced by their enrollment numbers. Citizens are voting with their feet. The performance scores do not make it a mystery of why parents are moving their children. We think the right thing to do is to give parents more options instead of being forced to move. It was a tough week for those seeking choices in their schooling options. Linnehan has an excellent bill to provide scholarships, but the state party is looking for more robust and bigger options in Choice to complement good bills like these. Fortunately, our Central Committee members have been working with support our legislative plan passed in August. The party fully supports investing in EVERY child (which includes private school kids). However, we have a difference of opinion on how to invest. We believe investments need to be placed into the hands of the parent and not a system with falling scores who have already proven their ability to steward more money for academic results (not working). Competition works. We trust parents’ investing choices for their most valuable asset...their children. an amazing bill to start to achieve that goal. Changes will happen to a bill to pass, but the bold vision and legal framework is there to make this happen. The Iowa legislature in conjunction with their Governor beat us to the punch again this week by passing out of committee a bill that would invest over 7k in funds to private school funds. This tips the scales of competitiveness back to Iowa. That can’t happen. Secondarily, the Parental Rights bill from Senator Murman was introduced. Parents and not systems have the final say in their children. is a BIG step towards reestablishing the right relationship between child, parent, and school system. The hearing for this critical bill is on the afternoon of January 31st. Be there! Finally, the SBOE continues to evaluate new candidates to lead the board of education. We are listening to our trusted Republican board members and watching these proceedings carefully. Our leadership has lived in a town with an ongoing trend of lower test scores and recently observed questionable teacher courses that must be observed by faculty. It is imperative all sides continue to work together to accomplish this expensive but very important goal. It is a monumental but important undertaking by the legislature, Governor, and PSC. name: Gene Karpinski, LCV
subject: URGENT: EPA, Interior Department, and Dept. of Energy -- all in jeopardy. We need your help.
body:
__Your impact goes three times as far when you renew today. |
---

|
DEVELOPING... as part of Kevin McCarthy's deal to become House Speaker, we've received word that Republicans will attempt to sabotage the federal agencies charged with implementing the Inflation Reduction Act, the historic climate bill signed into law last year.

The new House majority is using the debt ceiling as leverage to try to defund key agencies like the EPA, Treasury, Interior Department, and Energy Department -- places that are already facing staff shortages that could delay implementation of climate action and health and energy cost-saving benefits.****_****_

**We need to campaign like hell.** We're gearing up to drive thousands of letters and calls into Congress. We're rallying environmentalists in key states. We're strategizing with key Biden officials. And we're pressuring pro-climate senators to hold the line against any extremist bills coming out of the House.

**But to truly go toe-to-toe with corporate polluters in this fight, we need your help.**

We just kicked off our Membership Drive for 2023. This is the best way you can help right now. Can you fund our hard-hitting environmental campaigns by [renewing your LCV membership for just $5 today? For a limited time, every gift makes 3X the impact.](https://links.messages.lcv.)

| | **
** **League of Conservation Voters
2023 Membership Drive **

Supporter Record: Peter Willis
Match Status: [_TRIPLE MATCH ACTIVE_](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/yAyZM-)

Deadline: January 31, 2023
Fundraising Goal: $35,000

**Member Status:**[EXPIRED -- renew your membership today -- it only costs $5 right now](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/yAyZM-)**** |
---|---|---

Securing historic climate legislation was an _incredible_ victory, Peter. They said it couldn't be done. They counted us out. _But then we won._

**Don 't let Republicans hobble these hard-fought wins -- support our campaigns today.**

You can help _right now_ by [renewing your LCV membership for just $5. For a limited time, a group of generous donors will TRIPLE every gift to jumpstart our campaign.](https://links.messages.lcv.)

Thank you for everything you do to fight for a better and healthier future for our communities.

Gene Karpinski
President
League of Conservation Voters

---
**[**RENEW YOUR MEMBERSHIP**](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/-)**

---

| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/ContactInformation?spMailingID=9116002&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MTY2MDQ4MzY5NAS2&spReportId=MTY2MDQ4MzY5NAS2). name: Conservative Liberty Wire
subject: Watch the new free course now
body:
Our newest online course opens with the question, “What happened to my country?”

| This sponsored message from Conservative Liberty Wire is on behalf of Hillsdale College | | [](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=4a16f4bcd91e41338b5dfc4d294997d4&_e=6RyjGJ-tSlgyAswxqHST3icvCAJD_BwZuyEHTXCwskwmGbcAEH6GN1b-Ykx0jJOHcUsHu_ftl__EzA6EETcC3ccyimha5vrznNOZiE8Vqs2gPyYJYe4q-SBIxi_yOhYvI55at6uQhTAgY_45g2y8lYoMKdlUT3JNHwGNALLCV7v-NzC5bXbmwtLN3OeA-yaOJQG0NdPiKUpqnbCp66DC1u2QGWcp1rlUJ4xJy5_mi211xczVx84mwxJxqTImW4kaROQ3Tp2iru0bALCeBTYOZYoiSA-qSog0CKf327GcTNCykxVhgSWpDjS0NiBuw5yMLORbWYl8XHkvyhZaPr2NXLVQ3LYms3fxDTgghbkGYRP1rlod4P8VFwpauwS7XUBO8ueIXy4pIZTFYfgInnOqQA%3D%3D)
---
| | Dear Friend,

Our newest online course opens with the question, "What happened to my country?"

It's a question I've asked myself many times over the last few years as I've read the latest reports on radical gender ideology pushed in our elementary schools, speech squashed by the media and corporate class, basic rights left unprotected in many of our cities, and economic opportunity diminished for millions of Americans.

To understand the source of these radical changes that undermine the rights of American citizens, we've produced the new online course, **" The American Left: From Liberalism to Despotism."**
---
| | [](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=4a16f4bcd91e41338b5dfc4d294997d4&_e=6RyjGJ-tSlgyAswxqHST3icvCAJD_BwZuyEHTXCwskwmGbcAEH6GN1b-Ykx0jJOHcUsHu_ftl__EzA6EETcC3ccyimha5vrznNOZiE8Vqs2gPyYJYe4q-SBIxi_yOhYvI55at6uQhTAgY_45g2y8lYoMKdlUT3JNHwGNALLCV7v-NzC5bXbmwtLN3OeA-yaOJQG0NdPiKUpqnbCp66DC1u2QGWcp1rlUJ4xJy5_mi211xczVx84mwxJxqTImW4ka2TljQ3rQICYNLRhMEW9_WgTzw6mW7CwvaPdwpF6RQl4EH9knVWJ43yrjlLVwDhG6WBcpWF1Ff6GjEOr38O5p13EWXiipTWNGCK_C_N6pDYfbRdC8hP48YxJzqPeLMVJbl-jt0Xq-NU2I0WEANtUMiQ%3D%3D)
---
In this course, Dr. Kevin Slack, associate professor of politics at Hillsdale College, traces the transformation of the American left over the last 100 years. You'll learn how post-sixties radicals rejected the midcentury liberalism of Franklin Roosevelt; you'll discover the origins of modern gender ideology, identity politics, and today's global elite; and, you'll explore how these movements overtook America's key institutions.

You can watch the trailer for the course and preregister today using the secure link below:

**[https://lp.hillsdale.edu/american-left-preregister/](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=4a16f4bcd91e41338b5dfc4d294997d4&_e=6RyjGJ-tSlgyAswxqHST3icvCAJD_BwZuyEHTXCwskwmGbcAEH6GN1b-Ykx0jJOHcUsHu_ftl__EzA6EETcC3ccyimha5vrznNOZiE8Vqs2gPyYJYe4q-SBIxi_yOhYvI55at6uQhTAgY_45g2y8lYoMKdlUT3JNHwGNALLCV7v-NzC5bXbmwtLN3OeA-yaOJQG0NdPiKUpqnbCp66DC1u2QGWcp1rlUJ4xJy5_mi211xczVx84mwxJxqTImW4kaCDw4V4EyM7cucbXnd6eO2ecT_JAIEyb5WzdwcIAt9ZnvtDXQ22QIJYz9-Ctf-8oacVQhKQKi4mn6lyT2h2VsFS8PW1aeOtdlJecMPkaoLC9B-hbMXRkcHSC3RKL1at0ISgTrdG-T60eEQ0Mm2InwZQ%3D%3D)**

I hope you'll join us to better understand--and learn how to confront--the woke ideologies that dominate American politics today.
Best regards,

Kyle Murnen, '09
Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College

clean_body: Our newest online course opens with the question, “What happened to my country?” | This sponsored message from Conservative Liberty Wire is on behalf of Hillsdale College | | | | Dear Friend, Our newest online course opens with the question, "What happened to my country?" It's a question I've asked myself many times over the last few years as I've read the latest reports on radical gender ideology pushed in our elementary schools, speech squashed by the media and corporate class, basic rights left unprotected in many of our cities, and economic opportunity diminished for millions of Americans. To understand the source of these radical changes that undermine the rights of American citizens, we've produced the new online course, " The American Left: From Liberalism to Despotism." | | In this course, Dr. Kevin Slack, associate professor of politics at Hillsdale College, traces the transformation of the American left over the last 100 years. You'll learn how post-sixties radicals rejected the midcentury liberalism of Franklin Roosevelt; you'll discover the origins of modern gender ideology, identity politics, and today's global elite; and, you'll explore how these movements overtook America's key institutions. You can watch the trailer for the course and preregister today using the secure link below: I hope you'll join us to better understand--and learn how to confront--the woke ideologies that dominate American politics today. Best regards, Kyle Murnen, '09 Director of Online Learning Hillsdale College Sent to Conservative Liberty Wire subscribers. 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314

|

| | Derek,

It’s been 50 years since the landmark decision in _Roe v. Wade_. But on a day that should be spent celebrating a monumental decision in the fight for women’s rights, this year is different – it marks the first year since the ruling was overturned by the Supreme Court.

We continue to see states around the country attempt to ban abortion after the Supreme Court’s decision. **_Here in Virginia, Republicans are trying to ban abortion after 15 weeks of pregnancy._**

We can’t let this happen. On _Roe’s_ 50th anniversary, it’s more important than ever that we do everything we can to protect women’s reproductive freedoms and codify _Roe v. Wade_ into law. **[_Add your name now to urge the Senate to safeguard abortion rights._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57652763/388658010/-566081413?refcode=mw_em_230122_1&ms=mw_em_230122_1&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Manassas&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZTQ5ODJkNi05NzlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NlBVkGCgueUCGFmnZDLzuUdZD6EdQEnaHcV0eSAIEDE=&emci=065970e8-ce99-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&emdi=fe4982d6-979a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&ceid=4568237)**

Abortion is health care, and it’s a fundamental, established right. Today, on _Roe v. Wade’s_ 50th anniversary, we’re counting on our grassroots supporters like you to show up, speak up, and help us protect access to reproductive health care.

**[_Sign our petition right now if you’re with us in the fight to codify Roe v. Wade into law >>_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57652764/388658011/-566081413?refcode=mw_em_230122_1&ms=mw_em_230122_1&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Manassas&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZTQ5ODJkNi05NzlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NlBVkGCgueUCGFmnZDLzuUdZD6EdQEnaHcV0eSAIEDE=&emci=065970e8-ce99-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&emdi=fe4982d6-979a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&ceid=4568237)**

Left to Right: Senator George Borrello, Senator Rob Ortt, Assemblyman Mike Norris
---
# [NYS GOP lawmakers visit North Tonawanda to share opposition of Gov. Hochul's natural gas ban proposal - WKBW](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001esSpjksxuInSLbP2BXcC-Lry5IYc2XlsuaAfxdhCfTGJI2X5JxSzNnNggwIO-NqC0K0ghvIzQni13Gn2Fazla00kEdeScusbr7IjVQ8dETu66cf6WY_J1xFb_8ip1mr1N8Cr0whMheyK-xXVGsrCEUO1cb8Os63PQW2WiBYzWtEZFC5l-M8UALGa43l7mYEgcPebbwijxUFib_BTuHaGDnBzLDpbNH5HlJtwB9NOzrqUyRSKX_vj5g7CQMPI3ejG8a5QSMrt5KnDR9Mh0wX_k-LfyFME0elNdR49PMRMzTU=&c=XxmzFtYtU50q90xnW9MtQRteqIkamQ8Hd-62zZjTgwglA0SbUX8pAg==&ch=tA68nt588kYhcwIGTKGXVBNLBTG_rwOooEWqHsxLowQswinPYVbt2A==)

New York State Republican Senate leader Rob Ortt made a visit to North Tonawanda, at Pane's Restaurant, along with Sen. George Borrello and Assemblyman Mike Norris of the Lockport district.
Their argument is that Hochul has made gas stoves "public enemy number one" and that ridding homes of gas stoves, for the sake of preserving natural gas, is detrimental to the pockets of New Yorkers: for both homes and businesses.
"This is not going to make people's energy and utility bills go down. It's not. It can't because we're going to have to spend $270 billion over 30 years. That's one estimate," Sen. Rob Ortt said. "'There needs to be an investment in the grid'. The grid today can't handle the investment load."
Senator Rob Ortt cited the record storm we had in December where people relied on gas to heat their homes. He said New York needs a diversified energy portfolio and that has to include natural gas.

He said New York needs a diversified energy portfolio and that has to include natural gas. | == | | | Happy Lunar New Year! | | Wishing a very happy and healthy Lunar New Year in the Year of the Rabbit to all celebrating. | | | From Social Media: | == | | | Rob Ortt is a Battle-Tested Leader Defending You in Albany! | | == | PAID FOR BY ROB ORTT FOR SENATE | | | | | | | Ortt for State Senate | PO Box 1279, North Tonawanda, NY 14120 | | Sent by mailto:rob@robortt.com in collaboration with

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: explaining our campaign debt
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
#
_Hi — I’ll explain more below but our (victorious!) campaign has a little bit of debt to pay off. We made some great progress before the end of the year, and we’ve got just a little bit left to go. If you could give just $5 right now to help us pay it down so that I can continue focusing my energy on serving in the Senate, I’d be very grateful. Here’s the link: **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-hassan-fetterman](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/49668?refcode=em20230122-8220&t=1&akid=8220%2E434096%2EBW6wKo)**_
Derek — the last few weeks of our campaign were a whirlwind. GOP spending against us skyrocketed, polls tightened, and pundits were doubting whether we could win.
My team put our heads down and focused on our final advertising investments to get us through the last few days of the campaign. We were making decisions with the information we had, hoping with all our might that the funding will come through.
Unfortunately, all of our funding _didn’t_ come through and now my campaign has a bit of a hole to fill to get our last bills paid. We’ve made a ton of progress in closing the gap and since you’re one of my most important supporters, I’m hoping that I can count on you to get us over the top.
**[Will you please give $5 or as much as you can to help us retire our debt? Your donation will be split between my campaign and John Fetterman’s, and I’d be grateful for anything you can give.](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/49668?refcode=em20221229-8220&t=2&akid=8220%2E434096%2EBW6wKo)**
**[Donate Now](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/49676?refcode=em20230122-8220&t=21&akid=8220%2E434096%2EBW6wKo)**
I’m proud of you and our campaign team and the work we did together. Now, we just have to finish this thing off.
Thank you for your help.
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-emails?refcode=em20230122-8220-footer&t=22&refcode2=8220_434096_BW6wKo&akid=8220%2E434096%2EBW6wKo)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/JohnFettermanPA/) | [](https://twitter.com/johnfetterman) | [](https://www.instagram.com/johnfetterman/)
Email us: info@johnfetterman.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Fetterman for PA, PO Box 6061, Pittsburgh PA 15211.

name: Derek Kilmer
subject: Remaining vigilant in the fight to defend reproductive care
disclaimer_text: Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer
body:
In the year 2023, many Americans are living in states where having reproductive care is a felony.
Peter,

_Roe v. Wade_ - a landmark decision that ruled in favor of women's health and bodily autonomy - was decided by the Supreme Court 50 years ago today.

But last year, the Supreme Court's decision to overturn _Roe v. Wade_ effectively upended years of progress at the hands of far-right judges. Already, the narrow Republican House majority has passed two laws to further their anti-choice agenda.

Peter: reproductive care is health care. No lawmaker or judge should be allowed to weigh in on women's personal health care decisions. People all across the nation have been relying on _Roe_ 's legal protections for decades.

But now that those long-held protections are no more, millions of Americans are living in states where seeking life-saving reproductive care is a felony punishable by up to life in prison. Meanwhile, other states - like Georgia and Ohio - have extremely restrictive limits on when a woman can legally access care.

We need to remain vigilant in opposing proposals that would limit care options for women or could threaten people's health and lives.

**[I will continue to stand alongside the majority of Americans who firmly believe that access to reproductive health care is a fundamental right and is a decision that should be left between a woman and her doctor. If you agree, join me in urging Congress to codify _Roe_ by adding your name to my petition.](https://act.myngp.com/el/aBDXnnYMYXsH21Xzgqya0uaYynsPI5Vk8D36-yKYhJ0=/QRqNXHIYj923eZ2tkAwFnZOfVnqYoflGlclWqks0i_s=?ms=em23_230122_roe_adv_gq_act___dk_ettx&refcode=em23_230122_roe_adv_gq_act___dk_ettx&source=em23_230122_roe_adv_gq_act___dk_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230122_roe_adv_gq_act___dk_ettx&utm_medium=email&em={{EmailAddress%2CDefaultTo%3D}}&fn={{FirstName%2CDefaultTo%3D}}&ln={{LastName%2CDefaultTo%3D}}&pc={{ZipCode%2CDefaultTo%3D}}&contactdata={{ContactData}})**

Thanks for your support. As always, it's an honor to serve you.

Derek

name: Jahana Hayes
subject: Would have been Roe’s 50th Anniversary
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57653178/388660368/-1295403277?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODk4NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYWIzMGIwNy05YzlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LygjJ7lFNWdn7igZMvXcL-SY996ZdNd8YWs8fuMMVi8=&emci=f91600e4-fd99-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&emdi=eab30b07-9c9a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&ceid=15529135) | Today would have been a celebration of 50 years of the Supreme Court decision Roe v. Wade upholding abortion access for millions of families had it not been overturned last year. My team and I are feeling a lot of mixed emotions. For the first time, this anniversary is being observed in a post-Roe world. We’re angry, we’re sad, and we’re also determined to do whatever we can to defend abortion access at the federal level. The fight to secure the right to reproductive healthcare and abortion is far from over, so whether you’re mourning today or recommitting yourself to the fight for justice, I want you to know that I’m standing right there beside you. We must also recognize that many parts of the country were already living in a post-Roe world before last summer’s decision in Dobbs v. Jackson Women’s Health. Those communities, particularly Black and Brown communities in the south, are leading the way in our continued fight for reproductive justice. I am committed to doing whatever I can to ensure that every family has access to the care they need and to get the government out of these deeply personal decisions. Together, we will fight on, Thank you, Jahana Hayes
---

name: Team HER Time
subject: ABORTION IS HEALTH CARE
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the HER Time PAC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57653246/388660554/-2095412496?refcode=AU_HTP_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230122_PM1_V1_S2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDI1NDMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNmRmMDIwYzAtOWM5YS1lZDExLTk5NGMtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=dF9eINOKJqx06nA_XN_1Uq0w5K75YPo3BAcYCGQCW6A=)
---
**Abortion is health care. ** You know it. We know it. The people who don’t know it? Six unelected Supreme Court Justices, five of whom have never had a pap smear, five of whom were nominated by a President who lost the popular vote, two of whom have been credibly accused of sexual harassment or assault. We cannot allow these six people to have the final say about what we are allowed to do with our bodies. We at HER Time are actively standing up and fighting back in every way we can, and we’re asking you to stand with us. [_**Will you chip in $10 (or whatever makes sense for you!) to help us bring the fight for abortion rights to Congress? In Virginia, Democrats in the State Senate have held firm to protect women’s reproductive rights, but the GOP is dreaming about banning abortion in the Commonwealth. **We’re counting on you and our other grassroots supporters to[chip in and help us take the General Assembly back and block all anti-abortion bills in their tracks. **Welcoming Virginia Sheriffs’ Association**
---
---
---
**_This week the Virginia Sheriffs’ Association visited Richmond for their annual lobbying day where they spend time meeting with Members of the General Assembly. _** **Attorney General Miyares had the pleasure of welcoming them to the Office of the Attorney General. **
---
| |
---
**Empowering Workers, Supporting Virginia**
---
---
---
**Attorney General Miyares met with the Virginia Chamber of Commerce this week, highlighting the integral role Virginia’s business leaders play in providing employment opportunities which in turn supply workers with a renewed sense of empowerment and drive. ** _**Thank you**_**for the consistent support you bring to your communities and acting as one of the largest poverty-destroyers in the Commonwealth. **
---
| | **Advocating for Trafficked Minors**
---
---
---
**_Did you know that, in 2021, the majority of human trafficking victims were minors?_**
---
| |
---
|
---
| | **Thanks to those who joined AG Miyares this week for serious conversations about how to best advocate for trafficked minors and fight this heinous, multigenerational crime. **
---
**Combating Human Trafficking With Criminal Justice and the Medical Field**
---
---
**_Attorney General Miyares was honored to speak with medical students at Virginia Commonwealth University to discuss how to provide a trauma-informed and victim-centric response to our Commonwealth’s human trafficking issue. _**
---
| |
---
|
---
| | **Protecting Virginians’ Religious Freedom**
---
---
** _Attorney General Miyares enjoyed welcoming the Muslim Association of Virginia to the Office of the Attorney General on Tuesday. _**
---
---
---
**AG Miyares is committed to protecting the right of all Virginians to practice their faith in peace and without discrimination.**
---
---
**Henrico GOP**
---
---
**Attorney General Miyares also got to spend time with Henrico GOP to talk about his first year as Attorney General. **
---
---
---
---
---
[](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/u/click?_t=b7c680598d5648c3aaffcf8cea32abf4&_m=f97b219c920a4cfc9a63678e6af49fc4&_e=h3NZsl9qoNVAleLJghIaCbtrT5B56eI307LQjuDldE7RjvIeO7bm1UR1BVFa3hhVS_AuqotW9VN9U7y4a9b7rmfuHyHRKCp-3y-FWp32sGJt--yAabhNeX1EvqjbpKAw4SbrGjCPHieBpd264KfQshxLEEW1uuAIwPvFlqVhRjMSIPtP9hkEWknTMUs-3FbELgF_hSGxn-TcyB77VFDP1OWrOUKylNMLfxRdijO9cjw%3D)

_“One pill can take a life. One pill can rob you of your dreams. One pill can deny your family of seeing you again, of ever having a normal holiday because you had your life taken too soon. Part of our job is to remind people that mistakes will not define your life, that your past shouldn’t define your future, and that the biggest sign of strength if you’re dealing with addiction is just admitting that you need help.” _ **– Attorney General Jason Miyares** **Take the time to have the tough conversations with your loved ones regarding the fentanyl and counterfeit drug epidemic sweeping the nation. ** **You could save a life. **
---
Until next time, Team Miyares

clean_body: | | | Virginia – it’s been a busy year one. And the work is only getting started. "Year One - Attorney General Miyares" | | "Virginia Attorney General Miyares on America's Newsroom: Delayed Merit Recognition" Welcoming Virginia Sheriffs’ Association This week the Virginia Sheriffs’ Association visited Richmond for their annual lobbying day where they spend time meeting with Members of the General Assembly. Attorney General Miyares had the pleasure of welcoming them to the Office of the Attorney General. | | Empowering Workers, Supporting Virginia Attorney General Miyares met with the Virginia Chamber of Commerce this week, highlighting the integral role Virginia’s business leaders play in providing employment opportunities which in turn supply workers with a renewed sense of empowerment and drive. Thank youfor the consistent support you bring to your communities and acting as one of the largest poverty-destroyers in the Commonwealth. | | Advocating for Trafficked Minors Did you know that, in 2021, the majority of human trafficking victims were minors? | | | | | Thanks to those who joined AG Miyares this week for serious conversations about how to best advocate for trafficked minors and fight this heinous, multigenerational crime. Combating Human Trafficking With Criminal Justice and the Medical Field Attorney General Miyares was honored to speak with medical students at Virginia Commonwealth University to discuss how to provide a trauma-informed and victim-centric response to our Commonwealth’s human trafficking issue. | | | | | Protecting Virginians’ Religious Freedom Attorney General Miyares enjoyed welcoming the Muslim Association of Virginia to the Office of the Attorney General on Tuesday. AG Miyares is committed to protecting the right of all Virginians to practice their faith in peace and without discrimination. Henrico GOP Attorney General Miyares also got to spend time with Henrico GOP to talk about his first year as Attorney General. "AG Jason Miyares on Fox Business: 'Education is the doorway to the American dream'" "Virginia AG Jason Miyares on Fairfax County Schools Probe" | | "AG Miyares Expands Civil Rights Investigation Into Merit Awards Controversy in Northern Va. Schools" "One Pill Can Kill" “One pill can take a life. One pill can rob you of your dreams. One pill can deny your family of seeing you again, of ever having a normal holiday because you had your life taken too soon. Part of our job is to remind people that mistakes will not define your life, that your past shouldn’t define your future, and that the biggest sign of strength if you’re dealing with addiction is just admitting that you need help.” – Attorney General Jason Miyares Take the time to have the tough conversations with your loved ones regarding the fentanyl and counterfeit drug epidemic sweeping the nation. You could save a life. Until next time, Team Miyares | | ---|--- | | | | | | | Paid for and Authorized by Miyares for Virginia | | PO Box 3193, Virginia Beach, VA 23451 | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com | | |

| | She's one of many governors across the nation championing the effort to bring as many education choices as possible to parents and children, such as:
* **Education Savings Accounts**
* **Vouchers**
* **Scholarship Tax Credits**
* **Individual Tax Credits**
* **Public Charter and Magnet Schools**

Like our organization, Governor Reynolds understands that when parents have more options for their children's education – whether it's public, private, or homeschooling- it creates a better future for us all. name: Rick Larsen
subject: 50 years after Roe v. Wade, they want to ban abortion nationwide
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Citizens to Elect Rick Larsen
body:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57641862/388636951/-201694504?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZmFkMzlmZC03NjlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Mbr6faKkzX7jVFrFMUlsoUmaT9ao5e33TCGAbch4VJo=&emci=1dcfc399-0499-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&emdi=9fad39fd-769a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&ceid=9494239)
---

| | **Fifty years ago today, the Supreme Court stood up for reproductive freedom in their historic decision,_Roe v. Wade._** But instead of celebrating that milestone, I am gravely concerned about the future of safe and legal abortions across America in the wake of the _Dobbs_ decision that struck down _Roe._ If you are confused about where abortion is legal, under what circumstances, and what these bans mean for folks – _even here in Washington, where abortion remains legal_ – that is exactly what the radical anti-abortion forces want: chaos, confusion, and uncertainty. They are even trying to prosecute doctors, nurses, and patient advocates in pro-freedom states like Washington for helping women around the country get the care they need. _That is not OK._ **And the new, radical right-wing majority in Congress? They want to ban abortion nationwide. They have already forced a vote on it in the US House of Representatives.** I voted no, because my job is to protect your ability to make decisions about your body — and to keep the politicians, judges, and radical activists out of it. Abortions are deeply personal medical decisions that should be between someone who is pregnant and their doctor. **As long as I have the privilege of serving the people of Northwest Washington in Congress, I will stand for the right to safe and legal abortions.** _Rick_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | clean_body: | | | | Fifty years ago today, the Supreme Court stood up for reproductive freedom in their historic decision,Roe v. Wade. But instead of celebrating that milestone, I am gravely concerned about the future of safe and legal abortions across America in the wake of the Dobbs decision that struck down Roe. If you are confused about where abortion is legal, under what circumstances, and what these bans mean for folks – even here in Washington, where abortion remains legal – that is exactly what the radical anti-abortion forces want: chaos, confusion, and uncertainty. They are even trying to prosecute doctors, nurses, and patient advocates in pro-freedom states like Washington for helping women around the country get the care they need. That is not OK. And the new, radical right-wing majority in Congress? They want to ban abortion nationwide. They have already forced a vote on it in the US House of Representatives. I voted no, because my job is to protect your ability to make decisions about your body — and to keep the politicians, judges, and radical activists out of it. Abortions are deeply personal medical decisions that should be between someone who is pregnant and their doctor. As long as I have the privilege of serving the people of Northwest Washington in Congress, I will stand for the right to safe and legal abortions. name: Patty Murray
subject: Fifty years since Roe v. Wade
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PEOPLE FOR PATTY MURRAY
body:
The state of reproductive rights in 2023
#
Peter,
Today would have been the 50th anniversary of the _Roe v. Wade_ decision guaranteeing the right to abortion for women across the country. But instead of celebrating this milestone for the fundamental right for women to make their own health care decisions, we are in the fight for our lives to stop Republicans’ cruel, dangerous anti-woman agenda.
After decades of work to successfully overturn _Roe v. Wade_ last year, Republicans have made absolutely clear that they are not done yet. They want to ban abortion everywhere – including in Washington state – and they will not stop until they do.
**[Add your name to the petition if you’re with me in the fight to protect the right to abortion.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57653643/388662320/1979757337?refcode=pm_em_230122_1&ms=pm_em_230122_1&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MjU5NTM0Ny1hMTlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dSC9fVrKuz0h6wmsAe26vGRG4qVMSXoJmN3w9ADSLO8=&emci=492dbec3-929a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&emdi=52595347-a19a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&ceid=13116610)**
In the Senate, I am ready to do everything I can to stop the Republican-controlled House from advancing their anti-abortion agenda, while also working to get the votes we need in the Senate to restore _Roe v. Wade_ and safeguarding other critical aspects of reproductive health care – like birth control, IVF, family planning, and more.
This is the first generation of women who have fewer rights than their mothers and grandmothers. That is not ok – and it’s why I am fighting each and every day to restore every woman’s ability to control her body and her future.
**[We need all-hands-on-deck. Sign your name right now if you’re with me in the fight to protect abortion rights.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57653644/388662321/1979757337?refcode=pm_em_230122_1&ms=pm_em_230122_1&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MjU5NTM0Ny1hMTlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dSC9fVrKuz0h6wmsAe26vGRG4qVMSXoJmN3w9ADSLO8=&emci=492dbec3-929a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&emdi=52595347-a19a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&ceid=13116610)**
Thank you,
Patty Murray

subject: I’m officially running for re-election
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic Party of Georgia
body:
|
---

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57643029/388640091/227139380?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MGI3ODdjNy03YTlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TDj2whYOewi1Z4nhxkKe5F0tc0MaI4i61BccXotQCjg=&emci=d31ef681-d798-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&emdi=60b787c7-7a9a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&ceid=43566853)

| | _The Democratic Party of Georgia is focused on maintaining our Senate majority, retaking the House of Representatives, and holding the White House in 2024. Please take a minute to support Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand and protect our Senate majority._
I wanted to share this with you personally, Peter:

**I’m running for re-election to continue fighting for you and your family in the U.S. Senate. In a minute, I’m going to ask you to become a Founding Donor to my re-election campaign. But first, please let me explain why your support _today_ is so important.**

The truth is that the road to keeping this seat blue isn’t going to be easy. During the 2022 midterms here in New York, Republicans won more races across the state than they have in two decades — enough to deliver the House majority back to the GOP. Now, they’re using that momentum to target this seat as a pickup opportunity in 2024 so that they can retake the Senate, too.

But I’ve never backed down from a challenge. My very first race for Congress was in a Republican district where people said that there were “more cows than Democrats.” I still ran, and we won. That’s because I had grassroots supporters like you by my side from the very beginning, making the early investments that are key to building a winning campaign.

**So that’s what I’m asking of you today, Peter. With so much at stake, I’m counting on you to step up and become a Founding Donor today to help make sure I have the resources it will take to defend New York’s Senate seat and our Democratic majority.**

[**_Please, will you split $15 between my campaign and the Democratic Party of Georgia to become a Founding Donor to my re-election campaign to defeat the GOP in New York and defend the Senate for Democrats?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57643030/388640092/1480798934?refcode=20230122.1_KG_1X&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MGI3ODdjNy03YTlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TDj2whYOewi1Z4nhxkKe5F0tc0MaI4i61BccXotQCjg=)_

I’ve taken on some tough fights in the Senate: Securing health care for the 9/11 first responders. Repealing “Don’t Ask, Don’t Tell.” Making sure Americans got paid family and medical leave during the pandemic.

But there are so many critical fights ahead — for voting rights, for reproductive freedom, for health care, for a _permanent_ paid leave program. And I won’t let up until the fights are won. I promise you that.

**The GOP wants nothing more than to defeat me and halt progress on these critical issues, so I’m counting on your help to make sure we can take them head on and win.** [**_Please, Peter, will you split $15 between my campaign and the Democratic Party of Georgia to defend our Democratic Senate majority and make sure that I can keep fighting for you in the Senate?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57643031/388640093/1480798934?refcode=20230122.1_KG_1X&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MGI3ODdjNy03YTlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TDj2whYOewi1Z4nhxkKe5F0tc0MaI4i61BccXotQCjg=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

name: Dr. Kim Schrier
subject: 50 years since Roe v. Wade
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
50 years ago today Roe v. Wade became the law of the land. [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57647810/388642083/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NTFhOTNiMS03ZTlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=17sj1hH-tN4m3Bx893eh6IO35OO0gABR2pqaA84G1_Q=&emci=99590380-d998-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&emdi=751a93b1-7e9a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&ceid=15820338)
---
Peter,

50 years ago today _Roe v. Wade_ became the law of the land as the Supreme Court ruled that women had the right to make their own reproductive health care decisions.

But just last year, the Supreme Court undid decades of precedent by overturning this landmark decision. Since then, we've seen just how devastating this decision has been.

Immediately following the ruling, women in more than half the states in the country lost the freedom to make the most profoundly impactful health care decision of their lives – when and whether to have a child. And Republicans in state legislatures across the country have worked to pass more restrictive legislation.

To make matters worse, Republicans won back the House majority last November and have made it clear they're ready to roll back reproductive rights even further. They've already passed two anti-abortion measures and want to pass a national abortion ban.

As one of the few doctors in the House, I know that deeply personal reproductive health care decisions should be between a patient and her doctor, only. That's why I'll always fight back against these attacks on abortion care and work to ensure every woman has access to the reproductive health care she needs.

**[Are you with me? On the 50th anniversary of _Roe v. Wade_ , add your name now to join me in calling for the codification of _Roe_.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57647812/388642085/-486218336?ms=em23_230122_roe_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&refcode=em23_230122_roe_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&source=em23_230122_roe_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230122_roe_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=VUIlqnJOgFkOaDlPwSr7JdoHK5maXE31QY0EwgU5mIXNMYzpf135wBz/MqLjM9DRnORifaTcAxc2QoddYU11J/P0G/2Wym/MQpQQBUquXu8+uygMCfNwrWgfPVJ1p4uUgc8waRyEcXWOMUr88DOI1+V6daEazszgWrWtjQYel3I=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NTFhOTNiMS03ZTlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=17sj1hH-tN4m3Bx893eh6IO35OO0gABR2pqaA84G1_Q=&emci=99590380-d998-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&emdi=751a93b1-7e9a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&ceid=15820338)** | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57647814/388642087/-486218336?ms=em23_230122_roe_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&refcode=em23_230122_roe_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&source=em23_230122_roe_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230122_roe_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=VUIlqnJOgFkOaDlPwSr7JdoHK5maXE31QY0EwgU5mIXNMYzpf135wBz/MqLjM9DRnORifaTcAxc2QoddYU11J/P0G/2Wym/MQpQQBUquXu8+uygMCfNwrWgfPVJ1p4uUgc8waRyEcXWOMUr88DOI1+V6daEazszgWrWtjQYel3I=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NTFhOTNiMS03ZTlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=17sj1hH-tN4m3Bx893eh6IO35OO0gABR2pqaA84G1_Q=&emci=99590380-d998-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&emdi=751a93b1-7e9a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&ceid=15820338)

clean_body: 50 years ago today Roe v. Wade became the law of the land. Peter, 50 years ago today Roe v. Wade became the law of the land as the Supreme Court ruled that women had the right to make their own reproductive health care decisions. But just last year, the Supreme Court undid decades of precedent by overturning this landmark decision. Since then, we've seen just how devastating this decision has been. Immediately following the ruling, women in more than half the states in the country lost the freedom to make the most profoundly impactful health care decision of their lives – when and whether to have a child. And Republicans in state legislatures across the country have worked to pass more restrictive legislation. To make matters worse, Republicans won back the House majority last November and have made it clear they're ready to roll back reproductive rights even further. They've already passed two anti-abortion measures and want to pass a national abortion ban. As one of the few doctors in the House, I know that deeply personal reproductive health care decisions should be between a patient and her doctor, only. That's why I'll always fight back against these attacks on abortion care and work to ensure every woman has access to the reproductive health care she needs. | Thank you, Kim = Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress Contributions or gifts to Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress are not tax deductible. Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress PO Box 2728 Issaquah, WA 98027 United States

name: Katie Porter
subject: A few years ago, I wouldn’t have imagined I would write this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=16346%2E3422442%2EN6o8Hb)
Derek,
Today marks the 50 year anniversary of the Supreme Court’s _Roe v. Wade_ decision.
A few years ago, most of us wouldn’t have believed that the constitutional right to an abortion wouldn’t still be here. And I’m sure you remember the shock and sadness the majority of Americans felt last June when the Supreme Court decided to revoke our rights.
It still amazes me that my kids now have fewer rights than I did growing up.
Now, it’s time to take action—to restore the nationwide right to an abortion and to ensure the Supreme Court does not encroach any further on our rights.
**In the Senate, I’d be an advocate for revoking the filibuster and codifying the right to an abortion into law.** But that’s the bare minimum. For years, Republican politicians at every level of government have worked to diminish abortion access, forcing women to travel long distances and across state lines. We need to stand up to their attempts to control our futures.
When I was out talking with voters this fall, I heard a lot of Californians tell me _“well abortion will never be illegal here.”_ Let me remind you of the facts: If Republicans control Congress and the White House, they have said they will ban abortion nationwide.
**For abortion access, every single election and every single seat matters. I’m running for the Senate because I’m unafraid to stand up to extremists who try to take away our rights.**
If you’re with me, consider signing on or making a contribution today.
**[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/15818?refcode=EMAF22_1_ROE_20230122_V1_F&t=2&akid=16346%2E3422442%2EN6o8Hb)**
**[Endorse Katie ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4s-em-endorse?source=EMAF22_1_ROE_20230122_V1_F&t=3&akid=16346%2E3422442%2EN6o8Hb)**
Thanks for being in this fight.
Katie Porter

Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington. Our campaign is people-powered, we don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil or Big Pharma. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-footer?refcode=EMAF22_1_ROE_20230122_V1_F&t=4&refcode2=16346_3422442_N6o8Hb&akid=16346%2E3422442%2EN6o8Hb)**

50 years of Roe v. Wade

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57650640/388645015/-1526739943?amount=8&refcode=em_fr_2023.01.22_b1_yc_all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjUwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5Zjc4MGIyZi04MjlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wL5cZOGNMj-uO8QuU35t__nI90KBvvtdpugkBh0jXuc=)
Peter, today is the 50th anniversary of the historic _Roe v. Wade_ decision that gave women a choice — and a painful reminder that it has been six months since the Supreme Court stripped them of that choice by overturning _Roe_.
**[Right now, under Kevin McCarthy's leadership, House Republicans have introduced two anti-abortion bills that would criminalize abortion nationwide — punishing women and their doctors.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57650641/388645016/-1526739943?amount=5&refcode=em_fr_2023.01.22_b1_yc_all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjUwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5Zjc4MGIyZi04MjlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wL5cZOGNMj-uO8QuU35t__nI90KBvvtdpugkBh0jXuc=)**
As a pediatrician, I know firsthand that the government has no place in private decisions between a woman and her doctor. **And as a congresswoman, I promise you that I will fight to codify reproductive rights into law at the federal level, just as I did in the Colorado state legislature.**

**[We know Republicans won’t rest until they fulfill their extremist right-wing agenda, so we need your help, Peter. The best way we can defend the right to choose is by keeping pro-choice Democrats in Congress — and the fight to retake the majority starts right now. SCOTUS does not get the last word on our reproductive rights. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57654248/388664982/966991095?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZjg1N2YzOS1hYzlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LJ1mdyKtVmR8YKlCKt7Ap8v_5R32CTK6EzRpRpZcZRM=&emci=61bc5de8-989a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&emdi=ef857f39-ac9a-ed11-994c-00224832eb73&ceid=43402272) | Peter: Today, we should be celebrating the 50th anniversary of the Supreme Court’s landmark _Roe v. Wade_ decision. Instead, we’re mourning it. The Supreme Court’s decision to overturn _Roe_ was dehumanizing and dangerous. It does not reflect the will of the majority of Americans who deeply value maintaining control over their own bodies. **Here’s the truth:_We all know and love someone who has had an abortion_ – regardless of your political affiliation, religion, occupation, race, or zip code.** Those who have had abortions are not nameless, faceless people. They are our friends, neighbors, partners, colleagues, and family members. They are _us_ – and deserve the dignity, respect, and rights that the _Roe_ decision provided all of us 50 years ago today. Republicans will try to ban abortion nationwide. That is their goal. But hear this: Justice Alito does not get the last word about our bodily autonomy. Extreme Republicans will not be allowed to take away our reproductive freedom. My Democratic colleagues and I will not stop until reproductive rights are restored as the law of the land. And with the vast majority of Americans standing behind us, _we will not lose._ Thank you for standing with me in the fight for reproductive freedom. Yours, Becca Balint [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57654249/388664983/1063445104?refcode=E20083-20230122-RoeAnniversary-SB-AbortionReproductiveRights-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZjg1N2YzOS1hYzlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LJ1mdyKtVmR8YKlCKt7Ap8v_5R32CTK6EzRpRpZcZRM=)

name: Dan Goldman
subject: My week back in NY-10
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York**
body:
After two weeks of Republican chaos and dysfunction, it was great to be back home in NY-10. |
#
---
**[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57650891/388646037/1288870341?refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230122_PM1_V1_S1_VSP&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MDc1M2RmYS04NDlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dQ5e2Qs80T5G0NfsK7c0sYxWwSq5OMLExbQze3YiAc0=)** After all the Republican chaos, dysfunction and extremism we saw in my first two weeks in Washington, it was an incredible feeling to return back home to New York’s 10th Congressional District to listen to constituents, attend community meetings, and host my community inauguration, which unlike Washington, happened as scheduled. On Wednesday night — surrounded by my wife, my children, and my incredible colleagues and community leaders at the Brooklyn High School of the Arts — I had the honor of being sworn in as Congressman by Senator Chuck Schumer, our brilliant Senate Majority Leader, who is also now _my constituent!_ Peter, in the last few weeks, I have told you about all that I am doing to make good on my promise to be a relentless voice to defend our democracy for all Americans — especially in the wake of the lies, misinformation, and corruption that we’re already seeing from the MAGA Republican-led majority. Of course, that commitment is part and parcel of the work that I am doing to serve and give voice to my constituents — to pass progressive policies that will even the playing field and ensure that all New Yorkers can live with dignity and pursue the American Dream. So today, as part of our new approach of writing fewer and more substantive and informative emails, I want to tell you a little about what I’ve been up to in my first week as Congressman in my community — and about the first steps I’m taking to deliver for New Yorkers.
* * *
**The Public Housing Emergency Response Act** On Tuesday, I visited with the Red Hook Initiative, an outstanding community-based organization, and members of Red Hook East Houses Tenant Association — residents of the largest NYCHA housing complex in Brooklyn – both of whom have organized to advocate for better living conditions for their underserved community. In most NYCHA developments around New York City, including at Red Hook East, years of underinvestment have contributed to unacceptable living conditions for residents — from leaks, mold and exposed wiring, to heating and gas infrastructure that are barely functioning. My district has nearly 30 NYCHA developments, and it’s my solemn obligation to ensure that all of my constituents can have safe, dignified and comfortable living conditions for themselves and their families. **That’s why Congresswoman Nydia Velazquez and I got to work right away to re-introduce the _Public Housing Emergency Response Act_ , which would provide $32 billion in federal funds for NYCHA to make long overdue capital improvements.** This bill almost passed during the last Congress, and with Republican leadership’s backward priorities, it will be an uphill battle right now. But NYCHA residents are counting on us to see this through, and I won’t stop fighting for them.
* * *
**Improving our Health Care System** The COVID-19 pandemic exposed the critical gaps in our healthcare system that disproportionately impact low-income Americans and communities of color, and it reaffirmed that we must ensure that everyone — no matter their economic status — has access to affordable, quality health care. Part of what makes our city great is the New York City Health and Hospitals network, which works to provide care for poor, working and middle-class New Yorkers. This week, I had the pleasure of meeting with Dr. Mitchell Katz, the network’s CEO, and his staff at Gouverneur Health in the Lower East Side, which provides comprehensive care services for lower-income communities, to discuss how we can work together and ensure everyone in our community can access the care they need. In the aftermath of the pandemic, our city faces a mental health crisis that has contributed to a rise in homelessness and public safety concerns. **To help address that crisis, I announced this week that I will re-introduce the _Michelle Alyssa Go Act_ , which aims to honor her life by allowing mental health institutions with more than 16 beds to receive federal Medicaid funds to provide long-term mental health and substance abuse care for the most vulnerable Americans.** Current law prohibits those institutions with more than 16 beds for mental health to receive reimbursement from Medicaid, which disproportionately impacts poor and vulnerable residents. I was proud to stand with Rep. Jerry Nadler and former Rep. Carolyn Maloney, who introduced the bill last Congress, as well as city and state elected officials and community members, to advocate for this common-sense legislation. I have already begun to reach across the aisle to find Republican co-sponsors so we can get this passed this Congress. Stay tuned for more on this critical piece of the puzzle to address mental health illness for our most vulnerable communities.
* * *
Peter, after being sworn in Wednesday, I emphasized to my community that my door is always open. I am working to defend our democracy and to deliver for my constituents in NY-10, but that can only be done in partnership with our communities. **Your support is fundamental to the success of everything we’re working to accomplish in Congress. I’m honored that you’re a part of this team with me, and I’m grateful for a contribution of any amount that’s meaningful to you.** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57650892/388646038/1288870341?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230122_PM1_V1_S1_VSP&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MDc1M2RmYS04NDlhLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dQ5e2Qs80T5G0NfsK7c0sYxWwSq5OMLExbQze3YiAc0=)
